pokemon:spheal.name: Spheal
pokemon:spheal.summary: Spheal always travels by rolling around on its ball-like body. When the season for ice floes arrives, this Pokémon can be seen rolling about on ice and crossing the sea.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Spheal always travels by rolling around on its ball-like body. When the season for ice floes arrives, this Pokémon can be seen rolling about on ice and crossing the sea.
It rolls across ice floes to reach shore because its body is poorly shaped for swimming.
They can’t swim well yet, and they move much faster by rolling. When they’re happy, they clap fins.
It rolls across ice floes to reach shore because its body is poorly shaped for swimming.
It is completely covered with plushy fur. As a result, it never feels the cold even when it is rolling about on ice floes or diving in the sea.
Its body is covered in fluffy fur. The fur keeps it from feeling cold while it is rolling on ice.
It crosses the oceans by rolling itself on drifting ice. Fluffy fur keeps it warm when the temperature is below freezing.
Its body is covered in fluffy fur. The fur keeps it from feeling cold while it is rolling on ice.
During the season when drift ice approaches the shore, Spheal prefers living on the ice—where fewer predators lurk—rather than the land. Its fur retains heat superbly and resists harsh cold.
Spheal is much faster rolling than walking to get around. When groups of this Pokémon eat, they all clap at once to show their pleasure. Because of this, their mealtimes are noisy.
It rolls across ice floes to reach shore because its body is poorly shaped for swimming.
It rolls across ice floes to reach shore because its body is poorly shaped for swimming.
SPHEAL is much faster rolling than walking to get around. When groups of this POKéMON eat, they all clap at once to show their pleasure. Because of this, their mealtimes are noisy.
SPHEAL always travels by rolling around on its ball-like body. When the season for ice floes arrives, this POKéMON can be seen rolling about on ice and crossing the sea.
As it drifts among the waves, Spheal probes the sea. As soon as it spots prey, it informs the Walrein in its herd.
It crosses the oceans by rolling itself on drifting ice. Fluffy fur keeps it warm when the temperature is below freezing.
This Pokémon’s body is covered in blubber and impressively round. It’s faster for Spheal to roll around than walk.
It rolls across ice floes to reach shore because its body is poorly shaped for swimming.
They can’t swim well yet, and they move much faster by rolling. When they’re happy, they clap fins.
They can’t swim well yet, and they move much faster by rolling. When they’re happy, they clap fins.
Its body is covered in fluffy fur. The fur keeps it from feeling cold while it is rolling on ice.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Spheal always travels by rolling around on its ball-like body. When the season for ice floes arrives, this Pokémon can be seen rolling about on ice and crossing the sea.

pokemon:walrein.name: Walrein
pokemon:walrein.summary: Walrein swims all over in frigid seawater while crushing icebergs with its grand, imposing tusks. Its thick layer of blubber makes enemy attacks bounce off harmlessly.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Walrein swims all over in frigid seawater while crushing icebergs with its grand, imposing tusks. Its thick layer of blubber makes enemy attacks bounce off harmlessly.
It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.
Not only does its thick blubber keep it warm, it also protects it from attacks. It shatters ice with its prized tusks.
It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.
To protect its herd, the leader battles anything that invades its territory, even at the cost of its life. Its tusks may snap off in battle.
It swims through icy seas while shattering ice floes with its large tusks. It is protected by its thick blubber.
It shatters drift ice with its strong tusks. Its thick layer of blubber repels enemy attacks.
It swims through icy seas while shattering ice floes with its large tusks. It is protected by its thick blubber.
Its thick tusks are strong enough to shatter drift ice. They have been known to break, but they will grow back by the next year. The Hisui region is well known for these broken tusks.
Walrein’s two massively developed tusks can totally shatter blocks of ice weighing 10 tons with one blow. This Pokémon’s thick coat of blubber insulates it from subzero temperatures.
It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.
It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.
WALREIN’s two massively developed tusks can totally shatter blocks of ice weighing ten tons with one blow. This POKéMON’s thick coat of blubber insulates it from subzero temperatures.
WALREIN swims all over in frigid seawater while crushing icebergs with its grand, imposing tusks. Its thick layer of blubber makes enemy attacks bounce off harmlessly.
Walrein’s tusks keep growing throughout its life. Tusks broken in battle will grow back to their usual impressive size in a year.
It shatters drift ice with its strong tusks. Its thick layer of blubber repels enemy attacks.
Walrein form herds of 20 to 30 individuals. When a threat appears, the herd’s leader will protect the group with its life.
It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.
Not only does its thick blubber keep it warm, it also protects it from attacks. It shatters ice with its prized tusks.
It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.
It shatters drift ice with its strong tusks. Its thick layer of blubber repels enemy attacks.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Walrein swims all over in frigid seawater while crushing icebergs with its grand, imposing tusks. Its thick layer of blubber makes enemy attacks bounce off harmlessly.

pokemon:sealeo.name: Sealeo
pokemon:sealeo.summary: Sealeo often balances and rolls things on the tip of its nose. While the Pokémon is rolling something, it checks the object’s aroma and texture to determine whether it likes the object or not.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Sealeo often balances and rolls things on the tip of its nose. While the Pokémon is rolling something, it checks the object’s aroma and texture to determine whether it likes the object or not.
It habitually spins things on its nose. By doing so, it learns textures and odors.
Be it Spheal or Poké Ball, it will spin any round object on its nose with the greatest of ease.
It habitually spins things on its nose. By doing so, it learns textures and odors.
SEALEO live in herds on ice floes. Using its powerful flippers, it shatters ice. It dives into the sea to hunt prey five times a day.
It touches new things with its nose to test for smell and feel. It plays by spinning SPHEAL on its nose.
It has a very sensitive nose. It touches new things with its nose to examine them.
It touches new things with its nose to test for smell and feel. It plays by spinning SPHEAL on its nose.
Its white whiskers are very sensitive. Sealeo will balance Spheal on the tip of its nose, checking its scent and its feel to be sure the Spheal is healthy.
Sealeo has the habit of always juggling on the tip of its nose anything it sees for the first time. This Pokémon occasionally entertains itself by balancing and rolling a Spheal on its nose.
It habitually spins things on its nose. By doing so, it learns textures and odors.
It habitually spins things on its nose. By doing so, it learns textures and odors.
SEALEO has the habit of always juggling on the tip of its nose anything it sees for the first time. This POKéMON occasionally entertains itself by balancing and rolling a SPHEAL on its nose.
SEALEO often balances and rolls things on the tip of its nose. While the POKéMON is rolling something, it checks the object’s aroma and texture to determine whether it likes the object or not.
This Pokémon has a habit of spinning round things on its nose, whether those things are Poké Balls or Spheal.
It has a very sensitive nose. It touches new things with its nose to examine them.
Sealeo live on top of drift ice. They go swimming when they’re on the hunt, seeking out their prey by scent.
It habitually spins things on its nose. By doing so, it learns textures and odors.
Be it Spheal or Poké Ball, it will spin any round object on its nose with the greatest of ease.
It has a very sensitive nose. It touches new things with its nose to examine them.
Be it Spheal or Poké Ball, it will spin any round object on its nose with the greatest of ease.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Sealeo often balances and rolls things on the tip of its nose. While the Pokémon is rolling something, it checks the object’s aroma and texture to determine whether it likes the object or not.
